Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: My house has a small garden.
options: A. There is a small garden in my house.
B. There has a small garden in my house.
C. A small garden is my house.
D. My house is in a small garden.
questions.answer: A. There is a small garden in my house.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: No city in the world is more expensive than Tokyo.
options: A. Tokyo is the most expensive city in the world.
B. Tokyo is more expensive than any city.
C. Tokyo is as expensive as other cities.
D. Tokyo is not very expensive.
questions.answer: A. Tokyo is the most expensive city in the world.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: It is necessary for you to finish your homework.
options: A. You should finish your homework.
B. You must finish your homework.
C. You can finish your homework.
D. You may finish your homework.
questions.answer: B. You must finish your homework.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: Let’s go to the zoo.
options: A. Why don't we go to the zoo?
B. What about go to the zoo?
C. Shall we going to the zoo?
D. Do you want go to the zoo?
questions.answer: A. Why don't we go to the zoo?

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: The pharmacy is in front of the cinema.
options: A. The cinema is behind the pharmacy.
B. The cinema is next to the pharmacy.
C. The cinema is opposite the pharmacy.
D. The cinema is to the left of the pharmacy.
questions.answer: A. The cinema is behind the pharmacy.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: I like the living room best in my house.
options: A. The living room is my favourite room in my house.
B. My house has a nice living room.
C. I don't like other rooms, just the living room.
D. The living room is the best in the world.
questions.answer: A. The living room is my favourite room in my house.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: We will probably live in a houseboat in the future.
options: A. We might live in a houseboat in the future.
B. We must live in a houseboat in the future.
C. We should live in a houseboat in the future.
D. We are living in a houseboat now.
questions.answer: A. We might live in a houseboat in the future.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: He is a very good football player.
options: A. He plays football very well.
B. He plays football very good.
C. He is good at play football.
D. He likes playing football well.
questions.answer: A. He plays football very well.

questions.question: Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: What is your address?
Choose the sentence that has the same meaning: My sister is more creative than me.
options: A. I am not as creative as my sister.
B. I am more creative than my sister.
C. My sister is as creative as me.
D. I am the most creative.
questions.answer: A. I am not as creative as my sister.
